methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Silicon Quantum Computing, founded by Michelle Simmons, is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised A$50.4M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a fragmented peer universe of 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms, including later-stage competitors such as IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable in this case. The quantum computing peer set contains only 7 same-sector firms total, with insufficient overlap in stage and valuation tier—zero companies at the same Series A stage, one at an adjacent stage, and zero in a similar valuation band. A meaningful median requires a minimum of three comparable datapoints; relying on an all-sector median would introduce substantial cross-stage and cross-technology bias and is therefore not defensible.

Given the inapplicability of peer-median methods, no triangulated valuation range can be derived from the available data. The primary constraint is the absence of a sufficiently dense cohort of Series A quantum computing companies with disclosed valuations. Any forward valuation estimate would require either additional comparable funding rounds at the target stage or alternative methodologies such as discounted cash flow or venture capital method analysis—neither of which are supported by the provided dataset. The key uncertainty remains the appropriate valuation multiple for early-stage quantum hardware versus later-stage competitors in substantially different commercial phases.
